metrics.narrative: Consumer Cellular and Zycus co-built a detailed ROI business case across four working sessions (27 Mar, 6 Apr, 20 Apr 2026). The model was revised from $17M savings on $350M spend to $32M savings on $660M spend (Business Case Connect, 20 Apr 2026), driven by cycle-time reduction, 4.4 FTE recapture over 5 years, and AP invoice cost revised from $80 to $60 per invoice. The buyer pushed back repeatedly on aggressive assumptions ('the projected 6% savings appears to be an aggressive estimate,' 6 Apr 2026), showing real internal scrutiny rather than rubber-stamping.

champion.narrative: Abe Comarow (Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager) has driven the entire evaluation: scheduling AP, Infosec, Legal, and IT sessions, gathering internal business-case inputs, and telling the rep directly on 25 Jun 2026 that 'we are the front runner and need to continue hitting the mark.' He proactively surfaced the legal backlog risk rather than letting Zycus discover it cold, and coordinated attendee lists as recently as the 7 Jul 2026 readiness session — sustained, cross-functional advocacy.

competition.narrative: Zycus began as the 'dark horse' alongside Coupa and SAP Ariba (20 Oct 2025), narrowed to a 'final three' by 12 Mar 2026, and by 25 Jun 2026 Abe named only Coupa and Zip as still live, both having 'dropp their costs significantly' late in the cycle — a real but manageable threat given Zycus's declared front-runner status and the co-built $32M ROI case. SAP Ariba has not been named in any call since the Oct 2025 discovery session and appears to have faded, though it remains on the Salesforce Competitor field (a hygiene gap, not a live threat).

identify_pain.narrative: The 20 Oct 2025 discovery call established that Consumer Cellular finds NetSuite's procurement module 'unfit for purpose' due to missing contract hierarchy, limited approval flows, and AP challenges, with an exit strategy targeted before the 2027 NetSuite renewal. Pain was reconfirmed repeatedly: manual CSV conversions for retail orders, siloed sales data, manual coding of non-PO invoices, and standalone supplier-risk tooling (Upguard). Heather Williams (AP) and Jason Chan (Controller) both validated these pains directly in the 5 Jun 2026 AP/Finance session.

paper_process.narrative: Abe requested the MSA, Order Form, and draft SOW for legal review on 20 May 2026. By 25 Jun 2026, 'they have all contracts with legal' but review is backlogged 'due to a recent legal rep leaving,' estimated at 'a few weeks.' The 7 Jul 2026 readiness session confirmed legal will now review/redline the SOW and DPA, with Zycus to share phased-vs-big-bang implementation materials and SSO documentation to support that review. As of today (9 Jul 2026), review has run ~7 weeks with no confirmed completion date — the deal's central bottleneck.

economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO is the named funding-approval authority: the 27 Mar 2026 session confirmed the business case would be 'presented to the CFO for funding approval, targeting a go-live before the Netsuite contract renewal in 2027,' and Karson's 28 Apr 2026 next-step note set a CFO readout target of 8 May 2026. No Avoma-verified call shows a Zycus rep speaking directly with the CFO; all business-case work has run through champion Abe Comarow, with Jason Chan (Controller) joining the 5 Jun 2026 AP session as the closest finance-side contact.

decision_process.narrative: The process has moved sequentially through department-specific validation: AP/Finance (5 Jun 2026), Infosec/IT Asset Management (28 May and 5 Jun 2026), Legal (16 and 18 Jun 2026), and a cross-functional Implementation Readiness Session (7 Jul 2026, ~2 days ago) pulling in IT leadership (Brian Carter, Steve Loar, Ken Simmons, Michael Kelley). Champion Abe Comarow and Colin Taira coordinate scheduling across these tracks; the 7 Jul session confirmed legal redlining of the SOW and DPA is the next formal gate.

decision_criteria.narrative: Consumer Cellular confirmed a 'final three' shortlist at the 12 Mar 2026 CLM call, with ROI metrics needed 'to secure Zycus in final three.' Criteria span NetSuite integration ease, AI/Merlin capability, CLM flexibility (buyer explicitly wanted to avoid a 'rigid system similar to mTorris,' 12 Mar 2026), Infosec certifications, and cost versus Coupa and Zip. By 25 Jun 2026, Abe told the rep Zycus is the front runner, indicating Zycus is currently clearing these criteria.

engagement.top_event: Deal team to follow up with Marques Johnson (GRC) by June 26 to secure written InfoSec clearance and confirm no outstanding InfoSec blockers. Marques attended InfoSec/ITAM demos May 28 and June 5 but no formal sign-off is documented.

items.detail: Coupa and Zip are pricing aggressively ('dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk' per Abe 2026-06-25), creating late-stage competitive pressure. Abe requested updated pricing 'showcasing list cost and CC pricing' to benchmark Zycus against Coupa's discounted offer. If Zycus cannot justify value (ROI, AI differentiation, Netsuite integration ease) vs. Coupa's lower cost, the buyer may reopen price negotiations or flip back to Coupa. Risk is real but manageable — the $32M ROI case is co-built with the buyer and defensible (Colin: 'feels defensible' 2026-04-20), and AI/Merlin differentiation is validated. Mitigation: deliver updated pricing comparison by 2026-07-10, anchoring in ROI and total-cost-of-ownership (SaaS + services + integration) to justify premium vs. Coupa's upfront discount.

Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. Risk is execution / timing, not fit or engagement — Legal attended CLM demos and raised no objections. north_star_verdict.math: Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c = 2025-12-09, so the deal has sat 211 days in Shortlisted with no further stage advance logged in Salesforce despite RFP, business-case, and now implementation-readiness activity that reads far more advanced. CloseDate has moved four times: 2026-10-04 (original) -> 2026-03-20 (9 Jan 2026) -> 2026-05-31 (13 Feb 2026, slip 1) -> 2026-06-30 (27 May 2026, slip 2) -> 2026-07-31 (25 Jun 2026, slip 3). MSA/Order Form/draft SOW have been with Consumer Cellular's legal team since 2026-05-20 (about 7 weeks) with no confirmed redline-return date as of the 7 Jul 2026 Implementation Readiness Session. A privacy questionnaire response and DPA redline were exchanged as recently as today, 8 Jul 2026, showing the deal is genuinely live, but completing legal redlines, signature, and handoff inside the remaining 23 days is unrealistic; a fourth slip to late Aug/Sept 2026 is likely.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is the buyer-confirmed front-runner with legal, privacy, AP, and IT teams actively working the paperwork, but the 31 Jul 2026 close date (23 days out) is not credible given a 7-week-old legal backlog and a pattern of three prior slips.

flags: Salesforce Stage (Shortlisted) understates real progress: legal redlining, a CFO business-case readout, and a scheduled implementation-readiness session are already underway; update the stage so forecasting and resourcing reflect reality.

Pricing pressure from Coupa/Zip: Abe on 2026-06-25 stated they are 'dropping their costs significantly'; requested updated pricing comparison; risk: if Zycus cannot justify value, buyer may flip or demand discount

The economic buyer (CFO) has had no confirmed direct Zycus engagement since the 8 May 2026 readout; have Abe arrange a short CFO touchpoint or reference call before signature to avoid a late funding surprise.

Single-threaded: deal relies solely on Abe Comarow (champion); no second champion or executive sponsor identified (no Executive Sponsor or Champion Contact Role beyond Abe). If Abe's priorities shift or he exits, deal stalls. Build a second thread into Legal (Bobbie Reyes) or Finance (Jason Chan).
Confirm the CFO's funding sign-off in writing before finalizing the SOW: no update has surfaced since the 8 May 2026 readout target, and IT/Legal are moving faster than the budget-approval thread.

Correct the Salesforce stage: implementation planning, MSA/SOW-with-legal, and confirmed 'front runner' status show the deal is functionally at Negotiation, not Shortlisted; update SF so pipeline reporting and resourcing match reality.
EB/DM unconfirmed: no explicit Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified; Jason Chan (Controller) engaged but final budget authority unclear; CFO readout planned 2026-05-08 unconfirmed
Close date 2026-07-31 (23 days) not achievable: remaining steps (SOW scoping + Legal redlines + SOW sign-off) cannot compress into 23 days; 30-60 day slip likely to late Aug or Sep 2026

SOW scoping unscheduled: mentioned as 'setting up' on 2026-06-25 (13 days ago) but no call scheduled. SOW must be finalized before MSA/OF can be signed. Schedule SOW scoping by 2026-07-12 (this week) to unblock contracting.
Convert the SOW/DPA/legal tracks into one dated mutual close plan; three prior close-date slips (Mar->Jul 2026) show timing, not engagement, is the real risk.
Onboard Bill Salisbury on the NetSuite integration scope Steve Loar owned before the small CCI IT team becomes a rollout bottleneck (Task email, 6 Jul 2026; Avoma, 5 Jun 2026).
best_practice_check.summary: Legal is the critical path; proactively unblock it, close the pricing gap versus Coupa/Zip, and get direct executive access to the CFO before final sign-off.

customer_preference.evidence: Abe Comarow told the rep directly, 'we are the front runner and need to continue hitting the mark' (Next_Step__c, 25 Jun 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the declared front runner in a narrowing field. Coupa and Zip remain live and both cut pricing sharply in late June 2026; SAP Ariba, named at the original Oct 2025 discovery call, has not resurfaced since and appears faded. The incumbent, NetSuite's native procurement module, has already been judged unfit for purpose by the buyer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Consumer Cellular is a well-qualified, engaged deal with a declared front-runner status and a co-built $32M ROI case, but the paper process (legal review of MSA/OF/SOW) has stalled for ~7 weeks and is the sole real blocker to the 31 Jul 2026 close date.

deal_risk.text: Legal has held the MSA/Order Form/draft SOW since 20 May 2026 (~7 weeks) with no resolution date; a 30-60 day close-date slip is likely.
Coupa and Zip's late-cycle price cuts create pressure to discount or clearly justify Zycus's value versus cheaper alternatives before signature.

win_position.text: Champion Abe Comarow told the rep directly on 25 Jun 2026, 'we are the front runner,' and the deal has progressed into contracting mechanics (SOW/MSA/DPA with legal, implementation readiness planning) well beyond typical Shortlisted-stage activity.
A detailed, buyer-scrutinized $32M ROI business case was co-built over four sessions and is the vehicle for CFO approval, evidencing real economic buy-in.
Coupa and Zip both cut pricing significantly in late June 2026 (Next Step, 25 Jun 2026), creating a live risk that the buyer reopens commercial terms before signing.
No Zycus rep has directly engaged the CFO (the economic buyer); all business-case and approval activity flows through the champion, leaving executive-level risk unmitigated if Abe's influence wanes.

deal_momentum.text: A buyer email reply was received 8 Jul 2026 (1 day ago) and a substantive 90-minute Implementation Readiness Session was held 7 Jul 2026 (~2 days ago), confirming active two-way engagement.

The buying committee has widened recently to include Finance (Jason Chan), and IT/delivery leadership (Brian Carter, Steve Loar, Ken Simmons, Michael Kelley), signaling growing organizational commitment.

Two outstanding Zycus deliverables (NetSuite live-integration and search-genie recorded videos), first requested 18 Jun 2026 and reiterated 25 Jun 2026, remain undelivered as of today, risking a perception of slow follow-through.

customer_commitment.text: Abe Comarow has personally driven scheduling across AP, Legal, Infosec, and IT tracks and proactively flagged the legal backlog rather than letting Zycus find out cold.
The big-bang-vs-phased implementation decision remains unresolved as of the 7 Jul 2026 session, meaning full buyer commitment to scope and cost is not yet locked.

hard.ais_why:
• The 112-minute 1/8/2026 exec session generated 16 action items. Zero of them mention AI, Merlin, Agentic, Anna, or Intake. Karson Keogh's commitments are budgetary pricing, ROI and business case showing cycle-time reduction and savings, eProcurement and reporting deep dive, fine-tuning Abe's numbers against the demo, and a CLM review with legal: classical procurement evaluation depth, no AI evaluation depth.
• Buyer Abe Comarow's single attributed action item is sending alternative time slots if the proposed meeting times do not align. Matt Cottrell, Colin Taira, and Garry Hill have zero attributed action items. Across the largest call in the opportunity's history, no Consumer Cellular stakeholder asked an AI question or requested an AI demo block. That mirrors the rep's earlier note about the account being slow to adopt AI.
• Zycus has flagged AI as Neutral for the account. With 11 Avoma calls reviewed and the full action-item set of the exec session corroborating that posture, confidence moves to High. Transcript keyword review showed Merlin and AI tokens present in the file, attributable to Zycus rep framing, not to any buyer ask captured in the action items.

Coupa and Zip are pricing aggressively ('dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk' per Abe 2026-06-25), creating late-stage competitive pressure. Abe requested updated pricing 'showcasing list cost and CC pricing' to benchmark Zycus against Coupa's discounted offer. If Zycus cannot justify value (ROI, AI differentiation, Netsuite integration ease) vs. Coupa's lower cost, the buyer may reopen price negotiations or flip back to Coupa. Risk is real but manageable — the $32M ROI case is co-built with the buyer and defensible (Colin: 'feels defensible' 2026-04-20), and AI/Merlin differentiation is validated. Mitigation: deliver updated pricing comparison by 2026-07-10, anchoring in ROI and total-cost-of-ownership (SaaS + services + integration) to justify premium vs. Coupa's upfront discount.

Consumer Cellular Legal (Bobbie Reyes, Joseph Reiber, Kane) has held the MSA, Order Form, and draft SOW since 2026-05-20 (over 5 weeks) but is backlogged due to a legal rep departure. Abe stated on 2026-06-25 that redlines 'should take a few weeks,' but no progress update has been provided. Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. Risk is execution / timing, not fit or engagement — Legal attended CLM demos and raised no objections. Mitigation: offer a dedicated legal-support session with Bobbie Reyes and Joseph Reiber by 2026-07-14 to walk through redline questions, provide Zycus standard positions on jurisdiction/T4C/indemnification, and accelerate review; set a target for Legal to complete redlines by ~2026-07-21 (10 business days) so the deal can move to signature by late July / early August 2026.

The close date of 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible — the SOW scoping call to finalize implementation roles/resources/costs has not been scheduled, Legal redlines have been stalled for 5+ weeks, and the SOW must be agreed before the MSA/OF can be signed (per Zycus contracting memo). Remaining steps — SOW scoping, Legal redlines of MSA/OF/SOW, SOW sign-off by the deal owner's manager and AVP Global Delivery, MSA/OF e-signature — cannot realistically compress into 24 days. A 30–60 day slip is likely, pushing close to late August or September 2026. Risk is a Close Date Risk verdict, not a loss — the deal is healthy, engaged, and progressing, but the date is optimistic. Mitigation: finalize SOW scoping this week (by 2026-07-12), accelerate Legal redlines with proactive support (target completion by 2026-07-21), and reset buyer expectation to late August close.

Abe stated on 2026-06-25 that redlines 'should take a few weeks,' but no progress update has been provided. Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. Risk is execution / timing, not fit or engagement — Legal attended CLM demos and raised no objections. Mitigation: offer a dedicated legal-support session with Bobbie Reyes and Joseph Reiber by 2026-07-14 to walk through redline questions, provide Zycus standard positions on jurisdiction/T4C/indemnification, and accelerate review; set a target for Legal to complete redlines by ~2026-07-21 (10 business days) so the deal can move to signature by late July / early August 2026.

value.detail: The close date of 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible — the SOW scoping call to finalize implementation roles/resources/costs has not been scheduled, Legal redlines have been stalled for 5+ weeks, and the SOW must be agreed before the MSA/OF can be signed (per Zycus contracting memo). Remaining steps — SOW scoping, Legal redlines of MSA/OF/SOW, SOW sign-off by the deal owner's manager and AVP Global Delivery, MSA/OF e-signature — cannot realistically compress into 24 days. A 30–60 day slip is likely, pushing close to late August or September 2026. Risk is a Close Date Risk verdict, not a loss — the deal is healthy, engaged, and progressing, but the date is optimistic. Mitigation: finalize SOW scoping this week (by 2026-07-12), accelerate Legal redlines with proactive support (target completion by 2026-07-21), and reset buyer expectation to late August close.
